# UC-22.51.27 · OT Compliance Programme Posture and KPI Reporting

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Governance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We make one big scoreboard that shows whether every single rule in our cyber-safety book has fresh proof. If any rule is missing fresh proof, the board lights up red and we go and fix it before the inspector arrives.*

---

## Description

Aggregates evidence-currency and alert volumes from every NCA OTCC UC (22.51.1 through 22.51.26 and 22.51.28) into a clause-by-clause posture matrix and a programme-level KPI suitable for executive reporting.

## Value

OTCC inspectors and the OT cybersecurity steering committee both need a single-page view of the entire programme. Without this UC, posture reporting is a quarterly manual exercise; with it, posture is live and continuous.

## Implementation

(1) Build `otcc_clause_uc_map.csv` mapping each of the 27 OTCC clauses to its covering UCs (output by UC-22.51.1 through .26). (2) Schedule UC-22.51.27 daily. (3) Build the executive dashboard.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.51.27` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `otcc_clause="OTCC-1-9-1-1"`. Each row preserves (uc_id, clause, ot_control_family, last_evidence_at, evidence_count_24h, alert_count_24h, days_since_evidence, evidence_drought_flag, kpi_score). Dashboard `NCA OTCC – Compliance Posture` is the executive view: 28 OTCC clauses × their UC coverage × evidence-currency × KPI score.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Disable UC-22.51.3 for 4 days. UC-22.51.27 MUST flag clause OTCC-2-2-2-1 with `evidence_drought_flag=true` (no evidence in 24h) and route an alert to the OT cybersecurity steering committee.

### Negative scenario

An OTCC clause with daily evidence from its mapped UC(s) MUST NOT be flagged.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- All 27 prior NCA OTCC UCs (22.51.1 through 22.51.26 and 22.51.28) operational and writing to `index=audit_evidence`.
- `index=audit_evidence`, `index=notable`.
- Lookups:
  - `otcc_clause_uc_map.csv` — owned by Head of OT Security. The 28 NCA OTCC clauses with their covering UCs. Columns: `clause`, `control_family`, `covering_ucs` (pipe-separated mvfield). Maintained as a single canonical map. Example rows:

    ```csv
    clause,control_family,covering_ucs
    OTCC-1-2-1-1,governance,UC-22.51.1
    OTCC-1-5-1-1,governance,UC-22.51.2
    OTCC-1-7-1-1,governance,UC-22.51.25
    OTCC-1-9-1-1,governance,UC-22.51.27
    OTCC-2-1-1-1,defense,UC-22.51.3
    OTCC-2-2-1-1,defense,UC-22.51.4
    OTCC-2-2-3-1,defense,UC-22.51.24
    OTCC-2-3-1-1,defense,UC-22.51.6
    OTCC-2-3-2-1,defense,UC-22.51.9
    OTCC-2-3-3-1,defense,UC-22.51.10
    OTCC-2-5-1-1,defense,UC-22.51.11
    OTCC-2-5-2-1,defense,UC-22.51.12
    OTCC-2-5-3-1,defense,UC-22.51.5
    OTCC-2-8-1-1,defense,UC-22.51.26
    OTCC-2-9-1-1,resilience,UC-22.51.22
    OTCC-2-10-1-1,defense,UC-22.51.7
    OTCC-2-10-2-1,defense,UC-22.51.8
    OTCC-2-12-1-1,defense,UC-22.51.13
    OTCC-2-12-2-1,defense,UC-22.51.14
    OTCC-2-12-3-1,defense,UC-22.51.15
    OTCC-2-13-1-1,defense,UC-22.51.16
    OTCC-2-13-2-1,defense,UC-22.51.17
    OTCC-2-14-1-1,defense,UC-22.51.18
    OTCC-2-15-1-1,defense,UC-22.51.19
    OTCC-3-1-1-1,resilience,UC-22.51.20
    OTCC-3-1-2-1,resilience,UC-22.51.21
    OTCC-4-1-1-1,third_party,UC-22.51.23
    OTCC-4-2-1-1,third_party,UC-22.51.28
    ```

  - KV Store `otcc_22_51_27_exceptions`.

## Step 1 — Verify upstream evidence flow

Each of the 27 prior UCs must write to `audit_evidence` with their `uc=22.51.N,reg=NCA_OTCC,clause=OTCC-X-Y-Z-N` markers. Cross-check via:

```spl
index=audit_evidence reg=NCA_OTCC earliest=-1d
| stats latest(_time) AS last_evidence_at count AS evidence_count_24h BY uc clause
| sort uc
```

Ensure all 27 UCs are present.

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

The SPL aggregates per clause across covering UCs (some clauses are covered by multiple UCs, taking the max score).

**Schedule**:

```ini
[UC-22.51.27: OT Compliance Programme Posture and KPI Reporting]
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -30d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, email
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.51.27,reg=NCA_OTCC,clause=OTCC-1-9-1-1
action.email.to = ot-cyber-steering-committee@example.com,ciso@example.com
alert.severity = 2
```

Email-only governance alert with the dashboard link in the body.

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a)** All 28 OTCC clauses present in the clause map and each has at least one covering UC.

**(b)** Evidence is flowing for all 27 prior UCs.

**(c)** Positive control per `controlTest.positiveScenario`.

**(d)** Negative control per `controlTest.negativeScenario`.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Dashboard**: `NCA OTCC – Compliance Posture` (executive).

- **Row 1, KPI gauge**: programme KPI today, with target line at 95%.
- **Row 2, matrix**: clause-by-clause status with colour-coded KPI score.
- **Row 3, 13-month trend** of the programme KPI.
- **Row 4, top-5 evidence-droughts** with drill-through to the affected UC.
- **Row 5, top-5 alert-volume drivers** with drill-through.

**Quarterly OT Cybersecurity Steering Committee reporting**: a Splunk PDF export of the dashboard plus a narrative section authored by Head of OT Security. Archived in the GRC tool as quarterly evidence.

**Annual NCA OTCC self-assessment**: the dashboard plus the `audit_evidence` archive serve as the evidence basis. The Head of OT Security maps each clause to its UC × evidence × control-test outcome.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**Programme KPI mysteriously low** — most likely cause is one or more upstream UCs stopped writing evidence. Drill down via the evidence-drought panel.

**Inspector-week stress test** — run a dry run by simulating an evidence query for any random clause. The dashboard plus the `audit_evidence` index should answer in under a minute. If not, accelerate the relevant search.

**OT-specific failure modes**:

- **Splunk platform incident**: a Splunk outage during the audit window will mask all evidence. Maintain the cross-region warm-stand-by from your platform resilience runbook; UC-22.51.20 (BCP exercise) should include Splunk recovery.
- **Clause-mapping drift**: as NCA publishes new OTCC versions, the clause map must be re-mapped. Track via `data/regulations.json` versions and re-issue `otcc_clause_uc_map.csv`.
- **Programme-KPI gaming**: avoid the temptation to inflate evidence by writing empty events. The evidence schema must contain measurable fields; the SME panel reviews evidence quality quarterly.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup otcc_clause_uc_map.csv
| eval covering_ucs=split(covering_ucs, "|")
| mvexpand covering_ucs
| rename covering_ucs AS uc_id
| join uc_id type=left [search index=audit_evidence earliest=-30d uc=* | stats latest(_time) AS last_evidence_at count AS evidence_count_30d count(eval(_time > now()-86400)) AS evidence_count_24h BY uc | rename uc AS uc_id]
| join uc_id type=left [search index=notable earliest=-30d uc=* | stats count(eval(_time > now()-86400)) AS alert_count_24h BY uc | rename uc AS uc_id]
| eval days_since_evidence = round((now() - last_evidence_at)/86400)
| eval evidence_drought_flag = if(evidence_count_24h IS NULL OR evidence_count_24h = 0, "true", "false")
| eval kpi_score = case(evidence_drought_flag="true", 0, evidence_count_24h > 0 AND alert_count_24h = 0, 100, evidence_count_24h > 0 AND alert_count_24h > 0 AND alert_count_24h < 5, 90, evidence_count_24h > 0 AND alert_count_24h >= 5, 70, true(), 0)
| stats values(uc_id) AS covering_ucs avg(kpi_score) AS clause_kpi_score max(evidence_drought_flag) AS evidence_drought_flag latest(last_evidence_at) AS last_evidence_at sum(evidence_count_24h) AS evidence_count_24h sum(alert_count_24h) AS alert_count_24h BY clause control_family
| eval evidence_completeness = if(evidence_drought_flag="true", 0, 1)
| eventstats sum(evidence_completeness) AS clauses_with_evidence count AS total_clauses
| eval programme_kpi = round((clauses_with_evidence / total_clauses) * 100, 1)
| table clause control_family covering_ucs last_evidence_at evidence_count_24h alert_count_24h clause_kpi_score evidence_drought_flag programme_kpi
| sort - evidence_drought_flag - clause_kpi_score
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Programme KPI gauge (target ≥95%). (Panel 2) Clause-by-clause matrix sortable by KPI score. (Panel 3) 13-month KPI trend. (Panel 4) Top-5 evidence-droughts. (Panel 5) Top-5 alert-volume drivers.

## Known False Positives

1. **Scheduled-UC pause for known maintenance** — a UC may be intentionally paused during a Splunk platform upgrade. Suppression: maintain `uc_maintenance_windows.csv` excluding affected UC × time window.

2. **Brand-new UC not yet generating evidence** — a UC added today has no 24h evidence. Suppression: only flag drought if UC `verified_status_since` is older than 7 days.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `otcc_22_51_27_exceptions`.
